77 new episodes of DBZ airing every weekday at 5pm on Cartoon Network (began September 4th).



New DBZ on YTV (The episodes air when YTV receives them. The schedule is very fluid).



Viz will probably release future graphic novels uncensored. A press release is expected in a few weeks.



DBZ CCG Hummer Tour in full swing, currently in Southeast U.S.



Movie #4, #5, and #6 slated for U.S. release in 2001.



FUNimation has licensed Infogrames/GT Interactive as a developer of DBZ games for PS, PS 2, and Gameboy Color. Expect nothing until 2002.



(Last Update: 11/09/2000)



DVD (FUNimation):
Android DVDs
(December/January)
Bardock: The Father of Goku
(December/January)

VHS (FUNimation):
Bardock: The Father of Goku
(11/21/2000)
DB Movie #3: Mystical Adventure
(11/21/2000)

(12/7/2000)

(3/7/2001)

(2/9/2001)

(3/14/2001)

(12/7/2000)

(2/9/2001)

(1/15/2001)

(3/7/2001)

powered by SYSTRAN Translation Software






Current Features: Contest
Thursday - 11/09/2000

Mr.E @ 21:12 pst - Today's Poll...of DOOM...
You people are all racist...Nameks are people too. Saiya-jin completely annihilated every other race, which totally suprised me.

The poll of this confusing evening deals with a confusing topic...the president. Which guy should have his finger on the BUTTON? You decide!

SkullMac @ 17:44 pst - Episode Notes, Message Board Woes, and Other Ramblings
I may sound like I'm beating a dead horse, but there was yet again a bit of misinformation in today's episode. During Roshi's voiceover of Goku's past, he states that there were five years between the 21st and 22nd Budoukis. While I don't recall if this error appeared in the Japanese episode as well, I would like to clarify that there were three years between the two tournaments.

Several of you are reporting that your password is not being mailed to you (or the mailed password fails) when registering for the message board. While there is nothing I can do directly to fix this, I do have a few tips for those having troubles:
- Wait until our server upgrade on Saturday (if the server is under heavy load, the registration may fail)
- Triple check your e-mail address (if its incorrect, there is NO way to retrieve your password)

As for my other ramblings, they are about the second Trunks DVD, Prelude to Terror, which I received today. I'd like to echo earlier reports that the DVDs are improving greatly over time. To recap, the Trunks DVDs include subtitled recaps, intro themes, and credit themes. In addition to this, there are improvements in scripts, extra features, and name spellings. All in all, the upcoming Android DVDs are looking mighty appealing. As usual, we will encode and post about a dozen movie clips from this DVD.

Mr.E @ 15:58 pst - Quick Note...
Back in the Yahtzee post I made yesterday, FUNimation makes mention of "Frieza 5th form." Unless they are referring to Metal Furiza, there is NO 5th form, and it's a typo. Koola has a 5th form, not Furiza. That is all.

Mr.E @ 15:31 pst - DBZ Game in the Works!
Here's the big news embedded in FUNimation's latest newsletter:

Thank you so much for registering with Club Z-the official fan club for DragonballZ. Sorry we have not contacted you sooner. We want to give you the inside information on a regular basis about what is going on at FUNimation with DragonBallZ. We will keep you updated as things happen, starting right now with:

Two words: Video Game. That's right, Infogrames/GT Interactive is beginning work on DBZ Video Games. The games will be for Playstation 1 & 2 and Color Gameboy. Be prepared because the games are coming in 2001.

FUNimation has just acquired permission to produce and release Movies 4, 5 and 6 which are titled in the Japanese as "Super Saiyan Goku", "Cooler's Revenge" and "Attack! 1,000,000 Power Warriors". The Movies will be released to home video sometime in 2001.

Also new exciting DBZ Squeezies will be in the market soon. SS Goku, Vegeta and Piccolo are the featured Squeezie Characters. You can kick, punch, throw, drop or smash these guys and they will always be ready for battle.

DBZ Club Shirts will flash into stores December 1st. They are unlike any other DBZ shirts and are some of the coolest shirts you will ever lay eyes on. They will be available at Musicland, Hot Topic and Gadzooks.

We have received a lot of questions about where to find the new DBZ Long Sleeved Shirts that are out. These shirts are available at Target, Walmart, the DBZ Store and Kmart. (You have to look for the shirts at Kmart because they are not in plain sight, but these new shirts are at Kmart.)

Finally, Christmas is right around the corner and we at Club Z have a suggestion on how to drop a hint to friends and family about the DragonballZ stuff you want for Christmas.

Here's what you do to let your wishes be known:

1. Using your favorite browser, go to www.dbzstore.com. Pick your favorite products that you want for Christmas and add them to your shopping cart. (Don't worry, it can be a really long list and be sure to check the Christmas Specials.)
2. When you are finished collecting the items you want, make sure you are on the page where your cart is displayed and click on File, then click Save As. Save the file as cartadd.html in your My documents folder.
3. After you've saved the file, go back to your browser window and empty your cart. (You don't want to really place an order - yet.)
4. Open your email software and compose a new email.
5. Attach the file cartadd.html to this new email.
6. Send this email to all of your friends and family that need your Christmas list and make sure you include www.dbzstore.com and (877) 465-8669 in the email so friends and family will know exactly how to get your Christmas stuff.

We also have 12 great Christmas Packages already assembled for you. Click on the Christmas link to the left to view these.

If you need assistance with your list or have questions, please contact the DBZ Store using the above web address or phone number.

All items purchased in the DBZ Store are guaranteed to arrive in time for Christmas if orders are placed by December 13, 2000.

Thanks for your time DBZ Fans.

GT Interactive is a very professional, excellent company. I best know them for the game "Driver," though they have several other famous products. Note that no date is mentioned, but I wouldn't expect ANYTHING until 2002. That's a bit more than a year, but from what I've heard from my sources, it will be well, WELL worth it.

Wednesday - 11/08/2000

Kaio @ 19:54 pst - Updatin' Kaio!
Two updates today, folks: First off, two more of CDC's great strip, Little Saiyalings, have been added to the fan manga section. Additionally, four more strategies have been added to the card strategies subsection of the card game section. Additionally, a new subsection, titled the freeza saga, has been added to the card game section.

Mr.E @ 19:42 pst - BK Promo Figures Available from FUNimation Store...
Either Burger King didn't sell many toys, or they are still in production. FUNi's store is selling all of the toys in a set for under fourteen dollars. It's a limited-time offer, and these could easily be collector's items. I'll check on the availability of these...

Mr.E @ 19:10 pst - Yahtzee!
Merchandising!

It's Yahtzee DragonballZ style. This new game from Hasbro uses DBZ characters SS Goku, SS Trunks, SS Vegeta, Frieza 5th form, Android 17, Android 20, Cell and the infamous Dragonballs. Like traditional Yahtzee the game is played with 5 dice, but this game has a twist. The sides of the dice contain pictures of DBZ Heroes and Villians instead of numbers. A DBZ dice arena and gameboard also come with the game. This game can be played alone or with up to 4 players. Players roll the dice in an effort to achieve specific dice combinations such as 3 of a kind, any 5 heroes, 5 of a kind (Yahtzee), and many more. Unlike conventional Yahtzee, in DBZ Yahtzee when a desired combination of characters is achieved players collect tiles instead of marking down a score. Each tile has a value and seven of tiles contain a specific Dragonball. Each player wants to get as many Dragonballs as he can. But watch out for Cell, he can also collect Dragonballs. Enjoy DragonballZ Yahtzee and Good Luck!

Those DBZ condoms FUNi staffers were joking about aren't too far off...thanks to Zakkrusz for pointing this out.

Mr.E @ 17:24 pst - Irwin 4-Pack...
More tidbits from Irwin care of figures.com:

New Series 14 Collector's Pack released!

The set comes with Nappa, Master Roshi, Garlic Jr., and Raditz. Despite the fact that the box says "Exclusive Master Roshi Figure", the Master Roshi figure, like all the others included in this pack, is the exact same one available on the individual card.

The Collector's Pack is only available at the Musicland family of stores (Media Play, Suncoast, On Cue, Sam Goody). It comes in the specialty silver packaging.

Just a new way of selling old figures...nothing extraordinary.
Tuesday - 11/07/2000

Mr.E @ 20:14 pst - The Republicans Own Our Souls...
I don't like the two party system...I especially don't like a one party system. Ah well, Bush wins. Site updates tomorrow (scans, some other goodies). Good night, everyone!

Update: Yes, I know the results changed overnight. Stop telling me... ;P I doubt there was a major miscount, but we'll have to wait and see. All hail President Florida!

Mr.E @ 20:14 pst - A Hold on Scans...
Some unexpected events have come up, and, as you have seen for the past few days, I can't maintain that scan pace. Do not worry, you'll see another update this week, but a bit less than expected (I was a bit ahead on the days I posted scans anyway). Oh and, SkullMac messed with the news good, didn't he? -_-

SkullMac @ 18:18 pst - She's Got The Look...
Some of you may have noticed PN is undergoing a few cosmetic changes. Fear not fans! We're testing a few things out at the moment, but nothing major.

Mr.E @ 15:40 pst - Blasting Figures Hit Canada...
Nice to hear...these are Irwin's new molds, so these figures are of considerably greater quality than the old lines and the Bandai figures. Here's what figures.com had to say:

Blasting Action Figures reach shelves!

The new Irwin Dragonball Z Blasting Energy action figures have begun to hit store shelves in Canada, and on the major auction sites. The assortment includes Super Saiyan Trunks, Imperfect Cell, Gohan, Krillin, and Frieza Form III. American distribution always seems a few weeks behind Canadian distribution with the new Dragnball Z figures, so look for them to start showing up in the states within the next few weeks.

Who knows, maybe I'll start collecting these things... ^_^

Mr.E @ 15:16 pst - Presidential Race...
Bush? Gore? Nader? All viable choices...but there's only one candidate you can trust...Mr.E. So go out there and make your country proud. The fat cats in Washington won't let me on the ballot, so you'll have to make me a write-in. There's still 5 hours left, and I'm sure at least a dozen of you are 18, so I have a fighting chance. Oh yes, my stand on the issues.: Abortions for some, miniature American flags for others. I believe people should be trusted with their own money...eventually they will see that I am most deservant of all that cash. And lastly, don't drink and drive. You might spill beer on those new trousers.
Monday - 11/06/2000

Kaio @ 22:02 pst - Terms!
A new subsection to the encyclopedia section has been added. This subsection, the terms section will most likely be most helpful to newbies, but it may help some more well versed fans who are still orienting themselves with DBZ. It contains basic terms used throughout DBZ or in DBZ discussions, most of which don't fit in to be mentioned anywhere else on the site. So check it out, and who knows, you might learn something new. The section will get larger as we are asked more term-related questions.

SkullMac @ 20:02 pst - 16 New DVD Movie Clips
Behold! 16 lovingly encoded movie clips from Ginyu - Double Cross have been added to our Movie Archive. The first Trunks DVD should be on my doorstep any day now, so I'll have clips from it within a week. On a related note, the download limit will be raised shortly after our server upgrade (which is schedule to happen in a few short hours). Lastly, I have perfected a method for capturing crisp DVD screenshots, so expect some of those shortly as well.

Update: I mistakenly refered to Ginyu - Assault when I ment to refer to Ginyu - Double Cross. Sorry for any confusion.

Mr.E @ 01:22 pst - More Bid For Power...
It's almost done everyone...not much longer now. ^_^ A whole mess of new screenshots have been added to the BFP site. When the mod is finally released, we will be mirroring it...so if you can't download it from them, we're sure to have a few slots open. Note: This *is* a Quake 3 mod...and will be a large file. Don't expect to play unless you have Q3 installed on your machine. Chris has promised a lot more multimedia showing BFP in action before the release, so expect oodles of movie clips and screen shots.
Sunday - 11/05/2000

Kaio @ 17:37 pst - It's Fanilicious!
Both the fanfic section and fanmanga section have been updated. The fanfic section has quite a few new chapters, but the update wasn't as big as I hoped it would be. Nonethless, it's worth a gander. The fanmanga section has only four pages added to the fanmanga titled Reflection, but I figured that I might as well do the updates at once.

Finally, all the MP3s in the Music Vault should be available. Miraculously, I wasn't disconnected while uploading today. Wangy.

Mr.E @ 10:53 pst - Sonny Strait Interview Posted...
They just keep coming and coming, eh? Jon "bloody knuckles" Allen has written up his *cough*EXCEEDINGLY*cough* long interview with the VA of Kuririn and Bardock, Sonny Strait. The dialogue contains Sonny's views on politics, the old VAs, and the manga. There's also several comics, and, as usual, a mess-load of MP3s. Check it out here

[Insert something clever and witty about Jon and Sonny's relationship here] [Insert insult aimed at Mike LaBrie here] [Mix with self-doubt, simmer]
Saturday - 11/04/2000

Mr.E @ 15:20 pst - Fanart!
It is done...An excess of SEVENTY wonderful images were added to our fanart archive. This batch is truely excellent, and being the cynical bastard I am, you know that must mean they're damn sweet. Since this update was so damn huge, I put 4 pieces in the title image on rotation. The artists who won the spot are (in no particular order): W.A.R, SSJMysticBejita, Brianna Garcia, and SkinwalkeR. Congratulations to all four who did an superb job...

I am still adding the artist information to the older images...bear with me, there is a LOT to copy and paste there. All the fanarts should be labeled by sometime next week. Enjoy the update, everyone!

Kaio @ 08:22 pst - Kornered Between a Rock and a Funny Place!
Your somewhat bi-weekly dose of hilarity has been updated at Kaio's Korner. You'll laugh until your stomach bleeds, and possibly until you become unconcious!

Mr.E @ 00:39 pst - Blue Sub Commercial Online...
Toonami has posted their promo for Blue Submarine No. 6 on their website. Fetch! Good dog! And I can't believe I neglected to post this until now, but Gundam Endless Waltz will be playing this upcoming Friday. If anything Skull "Georgia" Mac says is true, you can expect that movie to kick more ass than a redneck who finds his daughter in bed with a city boy **Hides his contusions**.

Mr.E @ 00:29 pst - New Series 17 Figure...
Looks like Irwin's latest line has expanded to include everyone's favorite lead character, Goku. See the images here. The Dragon Ball Toy Island gets the buttery pancake for catching this one.
Friday - 11/03/2000

SkullMac @ 18:58 pst - Movie Clip Status
I'm pleased to report that all but 16 of our movie clips are back online (we are currently lacking the Driving Episode and Afterlife Tournament clips), and the remainder will be up shortly. I'd also like to announce that you'll be able to get your grubby little hands on some more sexy DVD clips in the coming week. Lastly, we are aware the server has been running rather slow lately and has been giving some rather odd errors. Upon investigation, we found that our server was unable to handle everything which we were running, so we will be upgrading the processor late Monday night (And to any server geeks out there: Our RAM usage is fine, its just the processor that's maxing out.).

Mr.E @ 17:27 pst - Final Flash!
My god...they left an actual attack name in...incredible. It's in English in the original, so it's about damn time they made my life easier and gave the attack the same name. Perfect Cell's voice is quite good, a less British version of King Cold. And, the music still sucks. I feel like I'm watching a pornographic film with all the excessive grunting and cheesy BGM.

Mr.E @ 16:28 pst - A Request for Help...
Here I am, staring at our FAQ section thinking "what the bloody hell am I going to add to this?" Then it occured to me: Who better to ask than the frequent visitor? I'm asking for help...I need to know what you guys think would be helpful Q&A's to add to the FAQ section. The questions should be rather general, unless something specific is asked over and over again (Like "is there a DBAF?"). It's really difficult to gauge by just looking at my e-mail box. Contact me if you have any ideas (put "FAQ" as the subject)...thanks in advance!

Mr.E @ 16:20 pst - Desktops Updated!
Well, that was a long-time coming...the desktops sections has been gallerized (is that a word?), and there are about a dozen new ones for you guys to gawk at. Check it out here.

Also, 10 new scans have been added to cover today's daily scan update and the one I missed yesterday. My fanart update is *almost* ready...

Mr.E @ 13:05 pst - It's Poll Time...
The results of our last poll showed that the average DB fan dislikes FUNimation's choice of music. Of those that did appeciate it, the two favorites were Master P and Slayer. Dream Theater and Bootsy Collins are the only two groups I can tolerate, and neither scored very high (Most teenagers have never heard of either).

Our latest poll serves no useful purpose whatsoever: Which race do you think is the best designed, best implemented, and most likable?

Mr.E @ 10:20 pst - DBZ Hummer Appearances...
The traveling CCG hummer is making a few stops in the Southeast this week:

We at Club Z want to let you know that the Dragon Ball Z Collectible Card Game HUMMER is heading to the Southeast corner of the US this weekend. The Hummer will make stops in Charlotte, North Carolina on Saturday November 4th from 12-5pm at the Media Play store located at 8600 University City Boulevard.

You will have an opportunity to meet and get autographs from Goku, played by Sean Schemmel, and Cell, played by Dameon Clarke. Pictures with Goku will also be available, along with other activities.

Then on Sunday November 5th, catch the DBZ Hummer in Alpharetta, Georgia from 12:30 to 4:30 at the Media Play store located at 6440 N. Point Parkway.

For more info about the Collectible Card Game or to find out when the Hummer will be in your neighborhood go to:

www.DBZcardgame.com

If anyone goes, be sure to slap Sean silly for me. He owes me a fansound.

Friday - 11/03/2000

SkullMac @ 18:58 pst - Movie Clip Status
I'm pleased to report that all but 16 of our movie clips are back online (we are currently lacking the Driving Episode and Afterlife Tournament clips), and the remainder will be up shortly. I'd also like to announce that you'll be able to get your grubby little hands on some more sexy DVD clips in the coming week. Lastly, we are aware the server has been running rather slow lately and has been giving some rather odd errors. Upon investigation, we found that our server was unable to handle everything which we were running, so we will be upgrading the processor late Monday night (And to any server geeks out there: Our RAM usage is fine, its just the processor that's maxing out.).

Mr.E @ 17:27 pst - Final Flash!
My god...they left an actual attack name in...incredible. It's in English in the original, so it's about damn time they made my life easier and gave the attack the same name. Perfect Cell's voice is quite good, a less British version of King Cold. And, the music still sucks. I feel like I'm watching a pornographic film with all the excessive grunting and cheesy BGM.

Mr.E @ 16:28 pst - A Request for Help...
Here I am, staring at our FAQ section thinking "what the bloody hell am I going to add to this?" Then it occured to me: Who better to ask than the frequent visitor? I'm asking for help...I need to know what you guys think would be helpful Q&A's to add to the FAQ section. The questions should be rather general, unless something specific is asked over and over again (Like "is there a DBAF?"). It's really difficult to gauge by just looking at my e-mail box. Contact me if you have any ideas (put "FAQ" as the subject)...thanks in advance!

Mr.E @ 16:20 pst - Desktops Updated!
Well, that was a long-time coming...the desktops sections has been gallerized (is that a word?), and there are about a dozen new ones for you guys to gawk at. Check it out here.

Also, 10 new scans have been added to cover today's daily scan update and the one I missed yesterday. My fanart update is *almost* ready...

Mr.E @ 13:05 pst - It's Poll Time...
The results of our last poll showed that the average DB fan dislikes FUNimation's choice of music. Of those that did appeciate it, the two favorites were Master P and Slayer. Dream Theater and Bootsy Collins are the only two groups I can tolerate, and neither scored very high (Most teenagers have never heard of either).

Our latest poll serves no useful purpose whatsoever: Which race do you think is the best designed, best implemented, and most likable?

Mr.E @ 10:20 pst - DBZ Hummer Appearances...
The traveling CCG hummer is making a few stops in the Southeast this week:

We at Club Z want to let you know that the Dragon Ball Z Collectible Card Game HUMMER is heading to the Southeast corner of the US this weekend. The Hummer will make stops in Charlotte, North Carolina on Saturday November 4th from 12-5pm at the Media Play store located at 8600 University City Boulevard.

You will have an opportunity to meet and get autographs from Goku, played by Sean Schemmel, and Cell, played by Dameon Clarke. Pictures with Goku will also be available, along with other activities.

Then on Sunday November 5th, catch the DBZ Hummer in Alpharetta, Georgia from 12:30 to 4:30 at the Media Play store located at 6440 N. Point Parkway.

For more info about the Collectible Card Game or to find out when the Hummer will be in your neighborhood go to:

www.DBZcardgame.com

If anyone goes, be sure to slap Sean silly for me. He owes me a fansound.

Previous News

Disclaimers
Planet Namek is just a fansite, we did NOT create Dragonball, nor did we have any part in the production process.

©2000, Bird Studio/Shueisha, Toei Animation. Licensed by FUNimation Productions, Inc. All Rights Reserved, or as otherwise directed by FUNimation. This site is not meant to infringe on any copyrights, it is simply here to encourage people to watch the Dragonball series.

Although we did not create the series, we did take a lot of time to work on this site and bring you original files and information. Please honor our efforts by not stealing our files/layout from us. Thank you.

Web Page Design © Planet Namek

Best viewed in 800 by 600 resolution or better & thousands of colors or more.
For the best experience, set your browser to view images "better" rather than "faster."
